front: What is an array?
back: An array is a data structure that stores multiple values under one identifier.

front: What is the identifier in an array?
back: The name of the array.

front: What is an element in an array?
back: An individual value stored in the array.

front: What must all elements in an array have in common?
back: They must all be the same data type.

front: What is an index?
back: The position of an element in an array.

front: What is the first index in pseudocode?
back: Usually 1, but it can be 0 or 1.

front: How do you declare a 1D array?
back: DECLARE Name : ARRAY[1:n] OF data type

front: Give an example of declaring a 1D array
back: DECLARE StudentNames : ARRAY[1:30] OF STRING

front: How do you declare a 2D array?
back: DECLARE Name : ARRAY[1:r, 1:c] OF data type

front: Give an example of a 2D array
back: DECLARE Grid : ARRAY[1:3, 1:3] OF CHAR

front: How do you assign a value to an array element?
back: array[index] ← value

front: What does names[3] mean?
back: The value stored at index 3 in the array names.

front: How can a variable be used as an array index?
back: A variable can choose which element is accessed, e.g. StudentNames[i] or Marks[row, column].

front: How do you access a 2D array value?
back: array[row, column]

front: What does marks[2,3] mean?
back: The value in row 2, column 3.

front: What is meant by traversing an array?
back: Going through every element in the array.

front: Why are loops used with arrays?
back: To process each element in the array.

front: What is meant by counting in an array?
back: Counting how many elements meet a condition.

front: What is meant by totalling an array?
back: Adding all values in the array.

front: What is a fixed-length structure?
back: An array has a set size that cannot change.

front: What happens if you access an index outside the array?
back: A runtime error occurs.

front: How are 2D arrays processed?
back: Using nested loops.
